data.title: Ralph Schackart Reiterates Buy on Alphabet, Citing Reaccelerating Search, AI-Driven Ad Innovation, and Margin Expansion in Google Cloud
data.description:
William Blair analyst Ralph Schackart has maintained their bullish stance on GOOG stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ralph Schackart has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Alphabet’s strong operating momentum and AI-driven execution. He highlights that Google Search reaccelerated, with record-high query volumes and notable enhancements in ad relevance, advertiser tools powered by Gemini, and new AI-centric ad formats that collectively expand the company’s ability to monetize more complex commercial searches.

Schackart also emphasizes the broad impact of Alphabet’s integrated AI stack, reflected in rapid uptake of Gemini-based products, robust growth in paid subscriptions, and meaningful traction in businesses such as Waymo. In addition, he points to the sharp acceleration and margin expansion in Google Cloud, a swelling backlog, and a disciplined but substantial investment roadmap—supported by higher capex guidance and a raised dividend—as underpinning his positive long-term view on the shares.

In another report released today, Pivotal Research also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $470.00 price target.

data.title: Analyst Reaffirms Buy on Broadstone Net Lease, Citing Solid Q1 Performance and Strong Cash Flow Visibility
data.description:
In a report released today, Eric Borden from BMO Capital maintained a Buy rating on Broadstone Net Lease, with a price target of $24.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Eric Borden has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight Broadstone Net Lease’s steady operating performance and cash flow visibility. He points to solid first‑quarter results, including year‑over‑year growth in adjusted funds from operations and same‑store rental revenue, fully collected rents with no bad debt, and successful asset sales at attractive cap rates and gains over purchase price.

He also notes that investment activity is on pace to meet annual guidance and that tenant metrics remain healthy, with stable restaurant rent coverage and improved financial reporting coverage across the rent roll. While leverage is somewhat higher than that of certain net lease peers and investment‑grade tenant exposure has ticked down, he appears to view these risks as manageable in light of the company’s consistent performance and supportive fundamentals, justifying a positive outlook on the stock.

data.title: Bobby Brooks Reiterates Buy Rating and Maintains $266 Price Target Following Revenue Beat and Robust Outlook
data.description:
In a report released today, Bobby Brooks from Northland Securities maintained a Buy rating on Bel Fuse Inc, with a price target of $266.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Bobby Brooks has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including a meaningful revenue beat in the first quarter and a robust outlook for the next period. The company’s top line exceeded both his and the Street’s expectations, margins came in at the high end of guidance, and free cash flow was solid, supporting confidence in the underlying earnings power.

He also highlights an above-consensus second-quarter sales forecast that reflects early benefits from acquisitions, particularly dataMate, and initial cross-selling wins that combine legacy Cinch offerings with Enercon products. In his view, the new segment structure, expanding defense design activity in Europe, and ongoing M&A and capital allocation opportunities provide a multi-year growth engine, justifying a reiterated Buy rating and an unchanged $266 price target.

data.title: Maggie Nolan Reiterates Buy on Cognizant as Large Deals, Acquisitions and Project Leap Drive Earnings and Margin Upside Potential
data.description:
William Blair analyst Maggie Nolan has maintained their bullish stance on CTSH stock, giving a Buy rating yesterday.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Maggie Nolan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Cognizant’s underlying momentum and improving profitability profile. While the first quarter showed only modest revenue growth and a slight margin shortfall, the company delivered solid EPS and benefited from strong large-deal activity, particularly in North America and financial services, which supports longer-term revenue visibility.

Management’s outlook for the second half anticipates acceleration as large contracts scale, recent acquisitions contribute, and discretionary demand gradually improves, offsetting near-term caution embedded in guidance. In addition, the cost-efficiency initiatives under Project Leap are expected to drive meaningful, recurring savings and margin expansion over the next few years, giving Nolan confidence that earnings growth and cash generation can outpace current expectations and justify a Buy rating despite a mixed near-term setup.

According to TipRanks, Nolan is an analyst with an average return of -10.3% and a 34.46% success rate. Nolan covers the Technology sector, focusing on stocks such as Exlservice Holdings, Accenture, and Cognizant.

data.title: Waystar: Buy Rating Backed by Strong Q1 Beat, AI-Driven RCM Leadership, and Attractive Risk/Reward Valuation
data.description:
William Blair analyst Ryan Daniels has maintained their bullish stance on WAY stock, giving a Buy rating on April 17.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ryan Daniels has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Waystar’s solid first-quarter performance and resilience amid softer industry volumes. Despite lighter procedure volumes from weather, flu patterns, and policy changes, the company exceeded expectations on revenue, adjusted EBITDA, and bookings, supported by robust recurring subscription growth that offset weaker volume-based sales.

Daniels also points to Waystar’s leadership in AI-driven revenue cycle management and its ability to expand its addressable market through new offerings and innovation, such as recent product launches and the Spring 2026 Innovation Showcase. He views the ongoing integration and cross-selling potential of Iodine as an additional growth lever, and with the stock trading around 10x his 2027 EBITDA estimate, he sees room for some multiple expansion and upside to forecasts, making the current risk/reward profile attractive.

Daniels covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Healthcare Services, TransMedics Group, and HealthStream. According to TipRanks, Daniels has an average return of -0.1% and a 42.52% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on April 17, J.P. Morgan also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $40.00 price target.

data.title: Analyst Reaffirms Buy on Hayward, Calling Post-Earnings Pullback a Buying Opportunity Amid Strong 2026 Outlook
data.description:
William Blair analyst Ryan Merkel has maintained their bullish stance on HAYW stock, giving a Buy rating on April 27.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ryan Merkel has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors including a strong first-quarter performance and what he views as achievable 2026 guidance. Despite the stock declining after earnings, he sees the pullback as a chance to purchase shares because channel inventories look healthy, Hayward is prudently conservative given macro uncertainty, and gross margins are expected to resume expanding in the second half.

He also believes Hayward is gaining market share and driving innovation, positioning revenue growth to beat peers in 2026 even if the overall pool market stays flat. Merkel views the company’s margin outlook as resilient, supported by operating leverage and fading SG&A investments, and notes that the shares trade at a modest valuation relative to long-term EBITDA potential, reinforcing his positive stance.

In another report released on April 27, Stifel Nicolaus also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $19.50 price target.

data.title: Analyst Reaffirms Buy on Yum! Brands, Boosts Price Target to $186 on Taco Bell Momentum and Strong Global Growth Outlook
data.description:
In a report released today, Andrew Charles from TD Cowen maintained a Buy rating on Yum! Brands, with a price target of $186.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Andrew Charles has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Yum! Brands’ operating momentum and Taco Bell’s outperformance. He highlights the first-quarter earnings per share coming in ahead of expectations, driven largely by Taco Bell’s strong same-store sales and effective execution across value offerings, beverages, marketing, and operations, which support an upward revision of Taco Bell’s long-term sales and margin outlook.

In addition, Charles emphasizes that management’s comments eased worries about geopolitical issues slowing KFC’s international expansion, reinforcing confidence in Yum!’s global development story. Reflecting this improved growth profile and Taco Bell’s enhanced profitability, he increases his 2026 same-store sales assumptions and lifts his price target on YUM shares from $180 to $186, supporting a continued Buy recommendation.

In another report released today, Barclays also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $185.00 price target.

data.title: Hannes Leitner Raises Price Target to $22.2 but Maintains Hold on Concerns Over Sustainability of Growth Drivers
data.description:
In a report released today, Hannes Leitner from Jefferies maintained a Hold rating on EDENRED, with a price target of €22.20.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Hannes Leitner has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including first‑quarter revenues that surpassed expectations thanks to stronger‑than‑anticipated savings on rebates and solid other revenues supported by elevated float and interest rates. While this demonstrates effective short‑term mitigation of the regulatory headwinds in Italy, it also highlights that part of the outperformance is tied to a favorable rate environment that may not be sustainable over the longer term.

Looking ahead, he expects regulatory countermeasures, higher face values, increased fuel prices and disciplined cost management to support EBITDA growth through 2026, leading him to lift his estimates above the company’s 8–12% guidance range and to raise his price target from $18.2 to $22.2. However, lingering concerns about the quality and durability of the underlying growth drivers, once temporary benefits fade, justify maintaining a neutral stance and therefore a Hold recommendation on the shares.

In another report released on April 24, Barclays also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a €21.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
